items.title: 'Predator' arsonist's $1.5m fire an 'act of retribution', court told

children.content: The guilty man embarked on a two-week crime spree after his relationship soured with his business partner, who he also assaulted in front of the man's children.

avatar.alt: A woman with blonde hair wearing a black blazer and white top

items.title: SA premier plays down social media posts attempting to reveal his home address

children.content: SA Premier Peter Malinauskas has played down social media posts attempting to reveal his home address in the aftermath of hundreds of trees being removed for the $45 million North Adelaide Golf Course.

image.altText: Premier Peter Malinauskas is wearing a suit and standing in front of new homes

items.title: Overseas company makes billions in profit but is receiving millions in government bailouts

children.content: China is "playing a hard game" with its stranglehold on the global minerals and metals market and Australian smelters need taxpayer support to "play hard back".

image.altText: Two large white buildings at a metal smelter by a port.

items.title: 'This is the end': SA health chief recalls terrifying moment she was hit by car

children.content: SA public health chief Nicola Spurrier opens up about the moment when she was knocked from her bike by a car last year, and recounted the experience — which left her with broken bones and fearing for her life — only hours after a 39-year-old motorcyclist was killed in a crash in the Adelaide Hills.

gallery.altText: A woman pulls the top of her blouse aside, showing a 5-centimetre scar on her left shoulder
a woman sits and smiles in a hospital bed, holding a cup with other food on a portable tray
A woman cycling with her helmet down a slope road with trees on the roadside

thumbnail.altText: A woman pulls the top of her blouse aside, showing a 5-centimetre scar on her left shoulder
a woman sits and smiles in a hospital bed, holding a cup with other food on a portable tray
A woman cycling with her helmet down a slope road with trees on the roadside

avatar.alt: A woman stands with her arms crossed, looking at the camera

items.title: Elk have been spotted in the wild in SA. Where did they come from?

children.content: A South Australian landowner has spotted elk on her property, outside containment lines of deer farms. There are no known established wild elk herds in this state.

image.altText: two large elk stand next to bushland.

items.title: PM 'confident' about Whyalla amid work to bring furnace 'back to life'

children.content: Anthony Albanese says he remains "very confident" in the future of steelmaking in Whyalla, and says the federal government is "continuing to work" with lead producer Nyrstar to secure its operations in Port Pirie and Hobart.

image.altText: A view of the Whyalla steelworks.

items.title: Steel and lead are facing trial by fire in SA's regional cities

children.content: The South Australian government has put all its chips on the table for the Whyalla steelworks and Port Pirie lead smelter. The next six months will be critical.

image.altText: A worker at the face of the blast furnace at the Whyalla steelworks.

items.title: Students of newly merged Adelaide University protest changing of graduation venue

children.content: The newly merged Adelaide University has changed the venue of its graduation ceremonies from Bonython Hall, to Pridham Hall with students launching a petition in opposition.

image.altText: Bonython Hall - an old heritage brick building in the Adelaide CBD

items.title: Stronger mouse poison allowed for 'worst plague on record'

children.content: Farm groups have welcomed the chemical regulator's long-awaited decision to approve double-strength mouse baits amid plague numbers in Western Australia and South Australia.

image.altText: Three mice in farm shed.

items.title: First Nations art collective calls for industry inquiry to protect artists

children.content: APY Art Centre Collective members say a voluntary code of conduct isn't protecting artists from financial exploitation and misconduct in the industry.

image.altText: The exterior of a building with corrugated metal sheets and a bright red logo, reading APY Art Centre Collective

tags.title: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Art

items.title: Welcome to campdraft school, where a new generation takes the reins

children.content: Amid the red dirt and flies of an outback stockyard, thrillseekers with a passion for horses learn about a quintessential Aussie sport.

image.altText: a cow running away from a girl riding a horse on red dirt with dust

items.title: Liberal senator speaks out against Taylor's budget reply

children.content: Senator Andrew McLachlan raises concerns over Angus Taylor's use of the term "mass immigration" and plan to push tax-paying migrants off welfare benefits.

image.altText: A man wearing a navy suit and tie with grey hair and a grey beard sitting in a grey room looks at the camera.

items.title: Fracking ban rethink on back-burner — but is it delaying the inevitable?

children.content: The SA government argues South East fracking would help unlock gas reserves at a time of limited supply — but it's a move that's vehemently opposed by many in the local community.

gallery.altText: Three men holding yellow signs with anti-fracking and gas messages on them

thumbnail.altText: Three men holding yellow signs with anti-fracking and gas messages on them

items.title: US conducts additional strikes against IS militants in Nigeria

children.content: The US Africa Command announced the strikes days after Trump claimed to have killed the group's second-in-command.

image.altText: black and white drone strike footage still. a group of people run in open ground

items.title: Inside the deadly search to recover four divers' bodies from a deep-sea Maldives cave

children.content: Expert divers have been sent to the Maldives to retrieve four bodies in a deep-sea cave network, in a search mission that's already killed one military rescue diver.

image.altText: A woman with blonde hair and a blue shirt sitting on a boat

items.title: Clare Wright wins top prize at NSW Literary Awards for 'powerful' history book Näku Dhäruk The Bark Petitions

children.content: The 1963 Näku Dhäruk or Yirrkala bark petitions led to Australia's first land rights law. A new book about them just won two prizes at the NSW Literary Awards.

gallery.altText: Clare Wright, 57, long blonde hair and glasses, arms folded, smiles brightly at the camera as she stands in a library.

thumbnail.altText: Clare Wright, 57, long blonde hair and glasses, arms folded, smiles brightly at the camera as she stands in a library.

avatar.alt: Hannah Story, a blonde woman sitting outside smiles. A dog is at her feet, and she wears a T-shirt that reads "MIRANDA JULY".

items.title: After another fatal shark attack, experts say risk can never be eliminated

children.content: The approach to tackling shark attacks has changed dramatically since the 2013 catch-and-kill policy, with a focus on shark tagging and tracking. But it's never enough to stop fatal attacks.

image.altText: A great white shark under water in the deep blue sea.

items.title: Blanchett says #MeToo reckoning in Hollywood 'got killed very quickly'

children.content: The Oscar-winning actor says Hollywood's reckoning over sexual misconduct and gender equality has stalled despite the global impact of #MeToo.

image.altText: A photo of Cate Blanchett posing at a film festival. She has short blonde hair and is wearing a black dress with a frilly neck.

items.title: How netball's 'super' veteran left retirement to break 12-year record

children.content: Romelda Aiken-George is supposed to be retired. Instead, the 37-year-old goal shooter is breaking all kinds of records and reinventing herself in her 19th season. After her 255th national league game on the weekend, she reveals the secret to her longevity.

image.altText: A player walks through a guard of honour and waves to the crowd with tears in her eyes

items.title: Horror MotoGP crash leaves rider with broken neck

children.content: Alex Márquez is lucky to be alive after he spectacularly wrecked his MotoGP bike during the Catalan Grand Prix in Barcelona, while Johann Zarco was also taken to hospital after an equally terrifying crash later in the race.

image.altText: Alex Marquez crashes

items.title: Five of our best recipes using the cheap and humble tinned bean

children.content: From vegetarian chilli to hearty soup, these easy recipes might convince you to add beans to your regular dinner rotation.

gallery.altText: Bowl of vegetarian chilli topped with sour cream, avocado, coriander, jalapeno, a vegetarian dinner that freezes well.

thumbnail.altText: Bowl of vegetarian chilli topped with sour cream, avocado, coriander, jalapeno, a vegetarian dinner that freezes well.

items.title: Meet the women rangers in Central Australia working to protect country

children.content: Dozens of Central Land Council Aboriginal rangers gathered east of Alice Springs last week for the 2026 Women's Ranger Camp — a time for health, learning and healing in the wake of the town's recent loss of Kumanjayi Little Baby.

image.altText: A group of Indigenous women sitting on the ground, stripping leaves from twigs.

items.title: How an Australian designer's fashion fame spurred a mental health unravelling

children.content: Rochelle Gregory has clothed everyone from Paris Hilton to Dua Lipa. Now the designer is using film to candidly explore her mental health battle while showcasing her latest collection.

image.altText: A woman in a black leather jacket struts down a runway while women clap.

items.title: Alice Springs' gruelling West Macs Monster ultra-marathon, in pictures

children.content: Over distances of up to 231km, 573 runners took to the Larapinta Trail outside Alice Springs at the weekend to compete in the 2026 West Macs Monster.

gallery.altText: A man runs along a ridge.
A group of runners in a pack.
Runners move along a dirt track.

thumbnail.altText: A man runs along a ridge.
A group of runners in a pack.
Runners move along a dirt track.

items.title: 'Boomer bashing': Are cuts to private health rebates addressing generational or wealth inequality?

children.content: The federal government plans to reduce private health insurance rebates for older Australians, a move one advocate fears could "tip many over the edge".

image.altText: An older woman in a grey jumper

items.title: Qld developer donation overhaul yields $170,000 in cash

children.content: More than $170,000 in donations from developers has been given to Queensland state politicians since laws banning developer donations were overturned in March.

image.altText: Jarrod Bleijie stands in front of an easel holding plans for a development.

items.title: Taiwan president responds after Trump's visit to China

children.content: Speaking after Donald Trump's meeting with China's president, Taiwan's leader says the island will not be sacrificed or traded and will not give up its free way of life.

image.altText: man smiles with green backdrop.

items.title: Some NT cases could halt if landmark legal challenge succeeds

children.content: Lawyers in the Northern Territory are commencing a landmark legal challenge that could require courts to halt some cases due to defendants being unable to access a lawyer amid a worsening legal aid crisis.

image.altText: A man with a serious expression sits on a long wooden table with legal books behind him.

items.title: A Canberra woman's husband said not to book return flights. It was a case of exit trafficking

children.content: Advocates for migrant women say they're seeing more cases of exit trafficking, a little-known crime involving coercion, threats or deception to organise a person's departure from Australia.

gallery.altText: A clip art image of a woman, passport and cancelled stamp

thumbnail.altText: A clip art image of a woman, passport and cancelled stamp

items.title: Port Kembla identified as preferred AUKUS base in secret report

children.content: A labour union has called for transparency after Port Kembla in NSW was identified as a preferred east coast location for a nuclear submarine base in newly released documents.

image.altText: A crowd of people standing on a submarine.

items.title: Church warns 'everything's on the table' as redress bill continues to climb

children.content: Tasmania's Anglican Church says what it expects to pay in compensation to victim-survivors of child sexual abuse has more than doubled in the past three years.

image.altText: A bishop standing in a church, looking at the camera.

items.title: Trial begins for concert pianist suing Melbourne Symphony Orchestra

children.content: Pianist Jayson Gillham is suing the Melbourne Symphony Orchestra in the Federal Court over political discrimination after comments he made on stage during a 2024 performance.

gallery.altText: Jayson Gillham sits on a step outside in a professional portrait photo

thumbnail.altText: Jayson Gillham sits on a step outside in a professional portrait photo

items.title: 'Bro, there's a frog in my lettuce': Woolworths investigates salad bag find

children.content: A Kiwi couple in a remote port town on WA's south coast have shared an unlikely discovery, a live frog in a bag of Woolworths lettuce.

image.altText: frog inside plastic bag with lettuce sitting on table

items.title: Man's leg amputated in public park, court hears

children.content: The trial of a man who allegedly performed an amputation on a 66-year-old, causing his death, is likely to focus on the accused's "state of mind" and intention, a court hears.

image.altText: A selfie image of a man wearing a black top on a tropical farm

items.title: Parent company orders cattle station to 'cease any plans' for bird cull

children.content: A cull of 20,000 native birds on a Northern Territory cattle station will not be going ahead.

gallery.altText: A little corella looks down from a gum tree
